description: The mathematical theory of finding extrema of functions subject to constraints. Encompasses continuous optimization (convex, nonlinear), discrete optimization, and algorithmic methods.

concepts.name: Unconstrained Optimization
concepts.description: Find min f(x) over x∈ℝⁿ. Necessary condition: ∇f(x*)=0. Sufficient: ∇²f(x*) positive definite (second-order condition). Local vs global optima. Functions with many local minima require global optimization.
references: Nocedal & Wright, Numerical Optimization, Ch.1; Boyd & Vandenberghe, Convex Optimization, Ch.9

concepts.name: Convex Function
concepts.description: f is convex iff f(λx+(1−λ)y) ≤ λf(x)+(1−λ)f(y). Equivalent: ∇²f ⪰ 0. Jensen's inequality: f(E[X]) ≤ E[f(X)]. Every local minimum of a convex function is global. Key property underlying convex optimization.

concepts.name: Convex Set
concepts.description: A set C is convex iff for any x,y∈C and λ∈[0,1]: λx+(1−λ)y∈C. Convex hull: smallest convex set containing S. Examples: halfspaces, polyhedra, balls, simplices. Intersection of convex sets is convex.

concepts.name: Convex Optimization
concepts.description: Minimize a convex function f over a convex set C: every local minimum is global. Standard form: minimize f₀(x) subject to fᵢ(x)≤0 (convex) and hᵢ(x)=0 (affine). Efficiently solvable in polynomial time (interior-point methods).

concepts.name: Linear Programming
concepts.description: Minimize cᵀx subject to Ax≤b, x≥0. Optimal at a vertex of the feasible polytope. Simplex algorithm: walks vertices; exponential worst case, polynomial in practice. Interior-point (Karmarkar): polynomial time. LP dual: maximize bᵀy, Aᵀy=c, y≥0.
references: Bertsimas & Tsitsiklis, Introduction to Linear Optimization

concepts.name: Quadratic Programming
concepts.description: Minimize ½xᵀQx+cᵀx subject to Ax≤b. Convex if Q⪰0. Active-set and interior-point methods solve QPs. SVM training is a QP. Generalizes LP; basis of sequential quadratic programming (SQP) for nonlinear programs.

concepts.name: Semidefinite Programming
concepts.description: Minimize C•X subject to Aᵢ•X=bᵢ, X⪰0 (positive semidefinite). Generalizes LP and QP. Interior-point methods solve SDPs in polynomial time. Used in control theory, combinatorial optimization relaxations, and matrix completion.
references: Vandenberghe & Boyd, Semidefinite Programming, SIAM Review 1996

concepts.name: Duality Theory
concepts.description: Lagrange dual: g(λ,ν) = inf_x L(x,λ,ν) where L is the Lagrangian. Weak duality: d*≤p* always. Strong duality (d*=p*): holds for convex problems under Slater's constraint qualification (strictly feasible point). Dual certifies optimality.

concepts.name: KKT Conditions
concepts.description: Necessary conditions for optimality in constrained optimization: stationarity (∇L=0), primal feasibility, dual feasibility (λᵢ≥0), complementary slackness (λᵢfᵢ(x*)=0). Sufficient for convex programs. Generalize Lagrange multipliers to inequality constraints.
references: Boyd & Vandenberghe, Convex Optimization, Ch.5; Nocedal & Wright, Numerical Optimization, Ch.12

concepts.name: Lagrange Multipliers
concepts.description: For minimize f(x) subject to g(x)=0: at a local optimum, ∇f = λ∇g (∇f parallel to ∇g). The system ∇f=λ∇g, g(x)=0 has n+1 equations for n+1 unknowns (x,λ). Generalizes to multiple constraints.

concepts.name: Gradient Descent
concepts.description: xₖ₊₁ = xₖ − αₖ∇f(xₖ). Converges for convex f with Lipschitz gradient: rate O(1/k). Strongly convex: linear (geometric) convergence. Step size by line search or fixed rule. Most widely used optimization algorithm in machine learning.
references: Nocedal & Wright, Numerical Optimization, Ch.3; Bubeck, Convex Optimization: Algorithms and Complexity

concepts.name: Stochastic Gradient Descent
concepts.description: xₖ₊₁ = xₖ − αₖ ∇fᵢₖ(xₖ) using a random sample index iₖ. Computationally cheap per iteration for large datasets. Converges with diminishing step sizes. Mini-batch SGD: average gradient over a random batch. Foundation of neural network training.
references: Bottou, Curtis & Nocedal, Optimization Methods for Large-Scale Machine Learning, SIAM Review 2018

concepts.name: Newton's Method for Optimization
concepts.description: xₖ₊₁ = xₖ − [∇²f(xₖ)]⁻¹∇f(xₖ). Quadratic convergence near a strong minimizer. Requires Hessian (expensive); quasi-Newton (BFGS) approximates Hessian from gradients. L-BFGS: limited memory variant for large-scale problems.

concepts.name: Principal Component Analysis
concepts.description: Find the k principal directions (eigenvectors of covariance matrix ΣX) maximizing variance. Equivalent to truncated SVD of the centered data matrix. Reduces dimension while preserving maximum variance. Applications in statistics, ML, and image compression.
references: Jolliffe, Principal Component Analysis; Bishop, PRML, Ch.12

concepts.name: Integer Programming
concepts.description: LP with integer (or binary) constraints on variables. NP-hard in general. Solution methods: branch and bound, branch and cut, LP relaxation + cutting planes. Applications: scheduling, network design, combinatorial optimization.
references: Wolsey, Integer Programming; Bertsimas & Tsitsiklis, Introduction to Linear Optimization, Ch.11

concepts.name: Dynamic Programming
concepts.description: Optimization method exploiting optimal substructure: optimal solution contains optimal solutions to subproblems. Bellman equation: V*(s) = max_a [r(s,a) + γV*(s′)]. Applications: shortest paths, knapsack, sequence alignment, reinforcement learning.
references: Bellman, Dynamic Programming; Cormen et al., Introduction to Algorithms, Ch.15
